title: Repurpose a Solidity variable after its original use to carry signals across assembly boundaries or reduce stack depth
trigger_patterns: variable no longer needed after assembly block
reuse parameter variable for derived value
reduce local variable count in assembly
description: YUL local variables consume stack slots. Reusing a variable whose original value is no longer needed avoids an extra stack slot and makes intent clear: 'this variable now means something else'. Commonly used to carry boolean flags out of assembly blocks into Solidity scope.
when_to_apply: When a variable's lifecycle ends before a new signal is needed, and the semantic shift is documented
when_not_to_apply: Security-critical code where variable reuse obscures auditing, when the original value might still be needed
